items.title: Sync Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When a target harness has diverged from Claude Code (user manually edited Codex's AGENTS.md), show a diff and let the user choose: overwrite, merge, or skip per-section. Currently users lose manual edits silently on next sync, which erodes trust and causes fear of syncing.

items.title: Visual Capability Gap Map
items.description: Show a matrix of what each harness supports vs. Claude Code features. E.g. 'Gemini doesn't support tool permissions — here's how we approximate it.' Helps users understand what they're actually getting in each harness instead of assuming parity.

items.title: Per-Feature Sync Toggles
items.description: Let users choose to sync only skills, only MCP servers, only rules, etc. per target. Power users want Cursor to get rules but NOT skills (different workflow), and currently it's all-or-nothing.

items.title: Reverse Sync: Import from Target
items.description: Pull config FROM a target harness INTO Claude Code. Users who started in Codex or Gemini before adopting Claude Code can bootstrap their Claude config from existing setups instead of starting from scratch.

items.title: Human-Readable Sync Changelog
items.description: Generate a natural-language summary of what changed in each sync: 'Added 3 skills to Codex, updated MCP server URL for Gemini, removed deprecated rule from all targets.' Users currently have no audit trail and can't explain to teammates what changed.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: Package the synced config state as a git-committable snapshot so teams can share a canonical harness config through their repo. Solves the 'my Codex setup is different from yours' problem on teams.

items.title: MCP Server Health Dashboard
items.description: Show real-time reachability and response time for all MCP servers across all target harnesses. Users waste time debugging 'why is Codex not using my MCP tool' when the server is just unreachable on a different port.

items.title: Skill Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which synced skills are actually invoked across harnesses and surface unused ones. Users accumulate skills over time and don't know which ones are stale dead weight vs. actively used.

items.title: Harness Context Switcher
items.description: One command to 'activate' a harness context — sets env vars, opens the right config, and shows a summary of what's synced to it. Reduces cognitive overhead when switching between coding tools mid-session.

items.title: Rule Contradiction Detector
items.description: Analyze synced rules across targets and flag semantic contradictions (e.g., Claude rule says 'always use TypeScript', Codex adaptation dropped that). Users discover these contradictions only when they get inconsistent AI behavior.

items.title: Auto-Detect Installed Harnesses
items.description: Scan the system on first run to detect which harnesses are installed and their versions, then offer targeted sync setup. Users don't know what's compatible and manually configured incorrectly causing sync errors.

items.title: Sync on Git Commit Hook
items.description: Auto-trigger sync when CLAUDE.md or plugin configs are committed to git, not just on file save. Ensures the synced state always matches the committed state — critical for teams using git as the source of truth.

items.title: Target Harness Version Pinning
items.description: Pin sync output to a specific version of a target harness's config format. When Codex ships a breaking config change, users want to stay on the old format until they're ready to upgrade, not have sync break silently.

items.title: Cross-Harness Skill Smoke Tests
items.description: After sync, send a standardized test prompt to each harness and verify the skill was picked up correctly. Currently users have no way to confirm a synced skill actually works in the target harness without manual testing.

items.title: Harness Migration Assistant
items.description: Guided workflow for users switching their primary harness (e.g., from Codex to Claude Code). Analyzes source config, maps features, identifies gaps, and generates a migration plan. Addresses the painful cold-start problem.

items.title: Secrets & Credential Scrubber
items.description: Before syncing, scan configs for API keys, tokens, or passwords and warn/redact before writing to target files. Users accidentally sync Claude Code settings containing secrets into less-protected Codex configs.

items.title: Sync Profile Presets
items.description: Save named sync configurations (e.g., 'work-strict', 'personal-experimental') that define different rule sets, enabled targets, and feature toggles. Users context-switch between work and personal projects with different risk tolerances.

items.title: Harness Performance Benchmarker
items.description: Run a standard task on all synced harnesses and compare response quality, speed, and cost. Helps users make data-driven decisions about which harness to use for which task type.

items.title: Config Dependency Graph
items.description: Visualize relationships between rules, skills, MCP servers, and which targets they sync to. Users with complex setups can't understand what breaks if they remove or rename a component.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Templates
items.description: When adding a new target harness, offer a curated template of rules/settings optimized for that harness's strengths. Codex excels at structured tasks — give users a head start instead of a blank slate.

items.title: Smart Incremental Sync
items.description: Use semantic diffing instead of file-level diffing — only re-sync sections that meaningfully changed, not every file that was touched. Saves users from overwriting manual tweaks because a comment changed.

items.title: Timed Snapshot Rollback
items.description: Automatically snapshot target configs before each sync and keep a rolling window (e.g., 7 days). Users who discover a bad sync three days later can roll back to before the issue, not just the last sync.

items.title: Sync Impact Cost Estimator
items.description: Estimate how many tokens/cost a full sync adds to each harness session (via larger context from more rules/skills). Users don't realize they're inflating their costs by syncing 50 skills to a pay-per-token harness.

items.title: Cross-Harness Feature Request Tracker
items.description: When HarnessSync can't sync a feature because the target lacks support, log it as a 'gap' and link to the target's issue tracker. Turns user frustration into actionable upstream feedback.

items.title: Config Quality Linter with Suggestions
items.description: Not just flag errors, but explain WHY something won't sync and suggest the closest valid alternative. Users get cryptic errors when their CLAUDE.md has syntax that has no target equivalent.

items.title: Harness Usage Heatmap
items.description: Track which harness you used for which file types over time and suggest optimal routing. 'You tend to use Codex for Python — consider syncing your Python rules first.' Helps users leverage each harness's strengths.

items.title: Plugin Ecosystem Bridge
items.description: Map Claude Code plugins to equivalent tools in other harnesses and auto-configure them. Users with a Sentry plugin in Claude Code want equivalent error-context available in Codex without manual setup.

items.title: Sync Status Badge Generator
items.description: Generate a README badge (like CI status) showing sync health across all targets. Makes sync status visible in repos and encourages teams to maintain healthy configs.

items.title: Conditional Rule Sync
items.description: Support rules with target conditions: 'sync this rule to Codex only' or 'exclude from Gemini'. Advanced users have harness-specific guidance they never want cross-pollinated.

items.title: Session Replay Across Harnesses
items.description: Take a Claude Code session transcript and replay the same prompts in other harnesses to compare outputs. Helps users evaluate whether it's worth maintaining a harness or consolidating.

items.title: Zero-Config Quickstart Mode
items.description: One-command setup that detects installed harnesses, infers sensible defaults, and gets users syncing in under 60 seconds. Current setup requires reading docs and manual config — most users abandon during setup.

items.title: Conflict-Aware Merge Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has been manually edited since last sync, detect the conflict and present a three-way merge UI (source, target, last-synced baseline) instead of blindly overwriting. Solves the pain of losing manual tweaks made directly in Codex/Gemini configs when the next sync runs.

items.title: Harness Preview Mode
items.description: Before syncing, render exactly what a target harness config will look like post-sync — including translated rules, resolved env vars, and omitted unsupported sections. Users currently have no visibility into what the sync will produce, leading to surprises. A /sync-preview <target> command closes this gap.

items.title: AI-Assisted Rule Idiom Translator
items.description: Use Claude to semantically translate CLAUDE.md rules into the native idiom of each target harness rather than verbatim copying. 'Always use TypeScript strict mode' means something subtly different to Gemini vs. Codex. This produces more native-feeling configs that the target harness actually respects.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Let users define multiple named sync profiles (work, oss, client-x) with different target sets, exclusions, and overrides. Switch profiles with /sync --profile work. Solves the problem of needing different sync behavior per project context without maintaining separate configs.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Onboarding
items.description: Detect when a new AI harness is installed on the system (polling ~/.cursor, ~/.gemini, etc.) and automatically run initial sync + show a 'New harness detected' notification. Eliminates the manual step of remembering to set up a new tool after installation.

items.title: Team Sync Templates
items.description: Allow teams to publish and subscribe to a shared sync configuration via a git URL or registry. One command adopts the team's agreed harness targets, exclusion rules, and override policies. Solves the 'everyone has slightly different sync setups' problem in engineering teams.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Report
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs, test each server's reachability and check whether the target harness supports its transport type (stdio/SSE/HTTP). Surface a clear report of which MCP tools will work, which will silently fail, and which need reconfiguration. Prevents phantom MCP tools that appear configured but never execute.

items.title: Skill Gap Analyzer
items.description: Compare Claude Code's installed skills against each target harness's skill system and produce a gap report: skills with no equivalent, partial mappings, and skills that can be auto-adapted. Helps users understand what they're giving up when switching harnesses and prioritize what to manually port.

items.title: Config Drift Alerts
items.description: Monitor target harness configs in the background and alert when they've drifted from what HarnessSync last wrote — indicating someone edited them manually or another tool modified them. Keeps users informed without requiring manual /sync-status checks.

items.title: Conditional Sync Rules
items.description: Support expressions that control sync behavior based on context: git branch name, project type (detected from package.json/Cargo.toml), or environment variable. Example: 'sync cursor rules only on feature/* branches.' Solves the problem of needing different harness configs for different workflow modes.

items.title: Per-Harness Overlay Files
items.description: Allow CLAUDE.gemini.md and CLAUDE.codex.md files to define additive overrides that get merged into the synced output for that specific target. Users can add harness-native instructions without polluting the master CLAUDE.md. Solves the tension between 'one source of truth' and harness-specific needs.

items.title: Sync Impact Score
items.description: When running a dry-run or preview, calculate and display an impact score: files changed, rules added/removed, MCP servers affected, estimated behavior delta. Helps users decide whether to sync now or wait, and surfaces unintended large changes before they happen.

items.title: Environment Variable Name Mapper
items.description: Maintain a mapping table of env var names across harnesses (e.g., ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs. CLAUDE_API_KEY vs. what Codex expects). Auto-translate env var references in synced configs rather than copying them verbatim. Prevents silent failures when a synced config references a var the target harness doesn't recognize.

items.title: Cross-Harness Session Handoff
items.description: Package the current Claude Code session context (active files, recent commands, task description) into a portable format and open it in another harness. /sync-handoff gemini would launch Gemini CLI with the equivalent context loaded. Enables seamless mid-task harness switching.

items.title: Harness Feature Parity Tracker
items.description: Maintain a live matrix of which features each harness supports (MCP, hooks, agents, skills, streaming, etc.) and update it when harnesses release new versions. Surface upgrade opportunities: 'Gemini CLI 2.3 now supports hooks — re-sync to enable.' Users currently discover capability changes by accident.

items.title: Compliance-Grade Sync Audit Log
items.description: Structured, append-only log of every sync operation: what changed, when, by which trigger (hook/manual/schedule), and the resulting state hash per target. Enables rollback to any point, supports security audits, and helps debug 'why did my Codex config change?'

items.title: Interactive First-Run Setup Wizard
items.description: A guided /sync-setup wizard that detects installed harnesses, explains what will be synced, lets users toggle targets and exclusions interactively, and performs an initial dry-run before committing. Reduces the friction of getting started for new users who don't want to read documentation.

items.title: Sync Webhooks
items.description: Fire configurable webhooks (or GitHub Actions events) when a sync completes, fails, or detects drift. Enables CI/CD pipelines to verify config consistency, send Slack alerts when syncs fail, or trigger downstream automation. Solves the 'is sync actually running?' problem in team environments.

items.title: Rule Effectiveness Insights
items.description: Track which CLAUDE.md rules Claude Code applies most frequently and surface insights: unused rules that could be removed, frequently-triggered rules that should be strengthened, and rules that never translate to target harnesses. Makes the rules file a living document rather than accumulated cruft.

items.title: Community Sync Recipes Registry
items.description: A curated registry of sync configurations for popular stacks (Next.js, Rust/Cargo, Python/UV, monorepos). /sync-recipe nextjs installs the community-recommended rules, exclusions, and harness mappings. Solves the blank-slate problem for new projects where users don't know what to sync.

items.title: Config Optimizer & Deduplicator
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and rules files for redundant, conflicting, or overly verbose instructions before syncing. Suggest consolidations that produce equivalent behavior with fewer tokens — reducing context overhead in every harness. Addresses the 'CLAUDE.md grew to 5000 lines' problem.

items.title: Plugin Portability Analyzer
items.description: For each installed Claude Code plugin, analyze how much of its value (commands, skills, hooks, MCP tools) can be replicated in target harnesses and produce a portability score. Helps users understand their 'Claude Code lock-in' and prioritize which plugins to build native equivalents for.

items.title: Harness A/B Testing Mode
items.description: Given a specific coding task description, run it through two harnesses with equivalent config and surface a structured comparison of the outputs. Helps users make informed decisions about which harness to use for specific task types and validates that synced configs produce equivalent behavior.

items.title: PR Merge Auto-Sync Trigger
items.description: Install a git post-merge hook that triggers sync automatically when a PR touching CLAUDE.md, .claude/, or .mcp.json is merged. Ensures the team's config changes propagate to all harnesses without anyone remembering to run /sync. Eliminates the 'I forgot to sync after the config PR landed' problem.

items.title: Universal Config Exporter
items.description: Export the current Claude Code configuration in any target harness format as a standalone file, without writing to disk. Useful for documentation, onboarding new team members, or sharing config snippets. /sync-export gemini > my-gemini-config.md gives a shareable artifact.

items.title: Per-Target Sync Schedules
items.description: Allow different sync frequencies per target harness. Codex config is sensitive — sync only on explicit command. Gemini can auto-sync on every save. Aider needs a daily refresh. Solves the all-or-nothing problem where users disable auto-sync entirely because one target is too noisy.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Warnings
items.description: Detect the installed version of each target harness and warn when syncing features it doesn't support yet, or when the harness is outdated enough to cause silent failures. 'Gemini CLI 1.8 doesn't support agents — upgrade to 2.0 or disable agent sync.' Prevents phantom configurations.

items.title: Multi-Workspace Sync Orchestrator
items.description: Sync Claude Code config across multiple project workspaces simultaneously — useful for monorepo users with multiple project dirs or consultants managing client environments. /sync --all-workspaces applies the current profile to every registered workspace in one operation.

items.title: Config Snapshot Gallery
items.description: Maintain a visual timeline of configuration states across all harnesses, with the ability to diff any two snapshots side by side. When something breaks, users can immediately see what changed between 'last known good' and now, rather than hunting through git history.

items.title: Natural Language Sync Query
items.description: Ask questions about your sync state in plain English: 'Which of my Gemini rules mention TypeScript?', 'What would change if I added Cursor as a target?', 'Show me everything that differs between my Codex and OpenCode configs.' Surfaces insights without requiring users to parse raw config files.

items.title: Harness Migration Assistant
items.description: When a user wants to switch primary harness (e.g., from Claude Code to Gemini CLI), generate a migration plan: what can be auto-converted, what needs manual work, and what will be lost. Produces a checklist and runs the conversion. Enables harness transitions without starting from scratch.

items.title: Daily Sync Digest
items.description: Send a configurable daily summary (terminal notification or file) of all sync activity: what changed, which targets drifted, any failures, and pending manual actions. Replaces the need to proactively check /sync-status by pushing a concise summary to the user's attention.

items.title: Permission Model Translator
items.description: Translate Claude Code's allowed/denied tools and permissions into the closest equivalent for each target harness, with explicit callouts where the mapping is imperfect. Prevents the security-relevant mismatch where Claude Code is locked down but Gemini gets an unrestricted equivalent config.

items.title: Sync Blame — Trace Any Config Line
items.description: Given any line in a target harness config file, trace it back to the exact source in Claude Code config that produced it, including the transform applied. /sync-blame gemini GEMINI.md:42 shows 'came from CLAUDE.md line 15, identity transform.' Eliminates 'where did this rule come from?' debugging.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
